com.mentor.chs.plugin

Interface IXPropertySetter

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXAttributeSetter

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXPropertySetter
    Interface for storing property changes to be applied to an object. This object can be obtained from an IXWriteableObject. Any change stored in this object is not applied until the IXAction.execute() exits returning 'true'.

    The last operation you decide to perform on a property is the only operation that will be applied. If in the IXAction.execute() method, you add a property, then later decide to remove it, the property will be removed when IXAction.execute() exits returning 'true'. Conversely, if you remove a property, then later decide to change its value, this time the property value will be changed when the IXAction.execute() exits returning 'true'.

    See Also:
    IXAction, IXWriteableObject

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addProperty(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String value)
      Used in custom actions to add a property to an object.
      java.util.Collection<IXValue> getProperties()
      Get a Collection of the properties on the object of this result
      java.util.Collection<java.lang.String> getPropertiesToBeRemoved()
      Get a Collection of the properties on the object that will be removed.
      void removeProperty(java.lang.String name)
      Used in custom actions to remove a property from an object.

    • Method Detail

      • addProperty

        void addProperty(java.lang.String name,
                 java.lang.String value)
        Used in custom actions to add a property to an object. This replaces any previously added property of the same name with the new value. If the property doesn't exist on the object, a new property will be created. If you call 'removeProperty()' then call 'addProperty()' for the same property, the property will be changed (the last method you call for a particular property will be the one that gets done).

        If the given property ‘name’ matches the name of an attribute on the object, then the attribute matching the given ‘name’ is changed to the supplied ‘value’, irrespective of whether there exists a property on the object with the same name or not. For example, consider a IXConnector instance whose "ShortDescription" attribute value is "current descr". If you call addProperty("ShortDescription", "new descr") on this object, then the short description attribute value of the connector is modified to "new descr"; a user property is neither added nor modified on this object.

        Parameters:
        name - - of property
        value - - of property (always a String)

      • removeProperty

        void removeProperty(java.lang.String name)
        Used in custom actions to remove a property from an object. If the property does not exist, this method will have no effect. If you call 'addProperty()' then call 'removeProperty()' for the same property, the property will be removed (the last method you call for a particular property will be the one that gets done).
        Parameters:
        name - - of property to be removed.

      • getProperties

        java.util.Collection<IXValue> getProperties()
        Get a Collection of the properties on the object of this result
        Returns:
        a collection of the properties on this result if any exist, an empty Collection otherwise.

      • getPropertiesToBeRemoved

        java.util.Collection<java.lang.String> getPropertiesToBeRemoved()
        Get a Collection of the properties on the object that will be removed.
        Returns:
        a collection of the properties to be removed on this result if any exist, an empty Collection otherwise.
